sound_type: loud rising 'curleee!' -- clear, ringing, rising whistle. Onomatopoeic origin of 'curlew.' Loudest call in Scolopacidae sequence (168-171). E=5
ecosystem: Open grasslands (breeding). Tidal flats, mudflats, agricultural fields (winter/migration). Largest shorebird in North America.
season: Migrant and winter visitor in PNW. Breeds on Great Plains grasslands. Small numbers winter on PNW coast.

coordinates.description: Long-billed Curlew on coastal tidal flats and estuaries of PNW during migration and winter. Also inland agricultural fields.

taxonomy_note.description: FOURTH SCOLOPACIDAE. Completes curlew comparison pair with Whimbrel (170). Longest bill of any North American shorebird (up to 21 cm). Largest North American shorebird. Bill morphology progression: straight > decurved > down-curved > extremely down-curved.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Scolopacidae: Wilson's Snipe (168), Dunlin (169), Whimbrel (170), Long-billed Curlew (171).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Numenius from Greek 'neoumenios' (new moon) -- curved bill shape. americanus -- of America. Curlew from the call: 'curleee!'
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Numenius -- curlews. Same genus as Whimbrel. Two Numenius now in engine (170, 171).
taxonomy_note.family_position: Fourth Scolopacidae. Second curlew. Extreme bill length. Curlew pair complete.

song_metrics.peak_energy_band_hz: ~2500 Hz at peak of rising call.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: ~0.5-1 second per call. Display song: extended series.
song_metrics.note_structure: Rising whistle 'curleee!' or 'cur-LEE!' Single-note rising sweep. Clear, ringing. Dramatic.
song_metrics.note_types: Rising 'curleee!' (primary -- territory/alarm/contact), display song (breeding -- elaborate flight display), alarm series.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Single rising call repeated at intervals. Display: extended series of rising and falling calls during flight.
song_metrics.timbre: Clear, ringing, whistled. Loud and projecting. Dramatic rising quality. Warm tonal character.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Very loud. Carries 1+ km on open grassland. Loudest in Scolopacidae sequence.
song_metrics.tempo: Individual calls repeated at 3-10 second intervals.
song_metrics.context: Territory, alarm, contact. Breeding grounds and migration. Open grassland projection.
song_metrics.key_property: RISING 'CURLEEE!' -- loud, clear, ringing, onomatopoeic. Longest bill (21 cm). Largest North American shorebird. E=5. v1.49.311.

plumage.overall: Warm cinnamon-buff overall. Long, extremely down-curved bill. Large, heavy body. Bright cinnamon underwings in flight (diagnostic).
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Extremely long, down-curved bill. Warm cinnamon plumage. Bright cinnamon underwings in flight. Very large size.
plumage.flight_pattern: Steady, direct flight. Bright cinnamon underwings diagnostic. Often calls in flight.

physical_description.overall: Length: 50-65 cm. Weight: 490-950g. Bill: up to 21 cm (8.5 in), extremely down-curved. Largest shorebird in North America.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Extremely long bill. Very large size. Cinnamon plumage. Cinnamon underwings.
physical_description.field_id: Very large shorebird with extremely long, down-curved bill. Warm cinnamon plumage. On tidal flats or grassland. Listen for loud rising 'curleee!'

nodes.title: Rising 'Curleee!' -- The Namesake Call
nodes.description: Loud, clear, rising whistle. Onomatopoeic origin of 'curlew.' Carries 1+ km on open grassland. E=5. Loudest call in Scolopacidae sequence. Dramatic rising quality.

nodes.title: Extreme Bill -- 21 cm, Longest in North America
nodes.description: Up to 21 cm (8.5 inches). Extremely down-curved. Longest bill of any North American shorebird. Females longer than males. Deep probing for ghost shrimp, crabs, earthworms.

nodes.title: Largest North American Shorebird
nodes.description: 50-65 cm length. 490-950g weight. Continental size record. Grassland-obligate breeder. Warm cinnamon plumage. Cinnamon underwings diagnostic.

nodes.title: Grassland Conservation Concern
nodes.description: Grassland-obligate breeder dependent on native prairie. Great Plains breeding habitat lost to agriculture. Near Threatened. Grassland loss is primary threat.

nodes.title: Curlew Comparison Pair Complete (170-171)
nodes.description: Whimbrel (170): 7-9 cm bill, E=4, rippling call, Arctic breeder, global range. Long-billed Curlew (171): up to 21 cm bill, E=5, rising call, grassland breeder, North American. Moderate vs extreme.

cross_references.relationship: CURLEW PAIR COMPLETE! Whimbrel (170): 7-9 cm, E=4, rippling. Long-billed Curlew (171): up to 21 cm, E=5, rising. Moderate vs extreme. Both Numenius.
BILL COMPARISON! Curlew bill curves DOWN. Godwit bill curves UP. Opposite curvature. Both long-billed. Both on tidal flats. Down vs up.
SCOLOPACIDAE STREAK! Four consecutive (168-171). Straight > decurved > down-curved > extremely down-curved. Bill morphology progression.
LARGE BIRD COMPARISON! Both very large. Sandhill Crane: tallest PNW bird, bugling call. Long-billed Curlew: largest shorebird, rising 'curleee!' Different size records.
